title: Reusable Workflows, Composite Actions, Interfaces, and Permission Flow · Delivery-System Trace: Ci Reusable Workflow Pin Provenance
summary: Teams copy release YAML across repositories; a shared workflow changes an input silently, accepts all secrets, escalates permissions, and hides which workflow revision produced an artifact. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci reusable workflow pin provenance increment in versioned reusable automation contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pin external reusable workflows, record workflow ref and SHA, verify producer identity, and update through compatibility tests.
Trace caller and called-workflow permissions, secrets inheritance, environment secret boundaries, nested depth, and non-escalation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.

  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example reusable workflow hides permissions and secrets, nests mutable dependencies, loses typed outputs, and silently changes callers. Design explicit inputs, outputs, authority, versioning, tests, and migration. Competency probe: Pin external reusable workflows, record workflow ref and SHA, verify producer identity, and update through compatibility tests. A defensible solution applies Ci Reusable Workflow Pin Provenance, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to versioned reusable automation contract.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-reusable-workflow-pin-provenance — Pin external reusable workflows, record workflow ref and SHA, verify producer identity, and update through compatibility tests.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-gha-reuse-composite-workflow-contracts-2
  - competency: ci-worked-gha-reuse-composite-workflow-contracts-2
    artifact: reusable-automation.yaml
    decision: worked gha reuse composite workflow contracts 2
    environment: trusted release tag requiring an environment approval
    fault-injection: route five percent traffic to a revision with a data incompatibility
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      component: reusable-workflow
      workflow-call: typed-inputs-secrets-outputs
      permissions: caller-bounded
      secret-inheritance: explicit-only
      environment-secret: deployment-job-only
      external-ref: full-workflow-sha
      producer-proof: workflow-ref-and-sha
      compatibility: consumer-contract-suite
      deprecation: measured-migration
    verify: validate caller and callee interfaces permissions secrets outputs pins nested depth changed consumer cases and rollback
    failure: Reject wrapper equivalence, implicit secret inheritance, permission escalation, default-branch refs, or centralization as drift elimination.
    repair: Choose the correct boundary, type the interface, preserve caller limits, pin identity, test consumers, canary change, and deprecate visibly.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Referencing a workflow on the default branch preserves reproducible delivery. This fails the Ci Reusable Workflow Pin Provenance contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
